summary: Soundboard recording of decent clarity with occasional tape hiccups captures a solid 1979 show highlighted by a stellar Black Peter, tight Scarlet > Fire transition and an unforgettable drum jam.
review: The SBD source provides generally clear audio, though listeners note some odd moments and a mid‑Black Peter drop‑out. Musically the concert is strong – fans rave about Black Peter, the seamless Scarlet → Fire flow, the Estimated Prophet → Eyes of the World sequence and a remarkable Egyptian‑beat drum jam, while a few songs (Peggy‑O, Not Fade Away) feel lackluster. Overall sentiment is upbeat despite some criticism of the setlist’s predictability and Keith's piano work.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers rate the show 4‑5 stars and praise key performances; recording is described as good with only occasional flaws, yielding a combined weighted score around 4.1.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally crisp SBD sound, but tape has weird moments and a noticeable dip in the middle of Black Peter; Jerry's vocal strain adds slight muddiness on vocals.

standout_songs: Estimated Prophet > Eyes of the World
Beat It On Down The Line
show_quality.setlist_flow: Typical late‑70s setlist (Shakedown, BEW, Scarlet > Fire, Estimated Prophet → Eyes, NFA, Black Peter, Around & Around) that many find predictable, yet the band’s tight transitions and improvisational peaks keep the performance engaging.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Voice ragged/under‑the‑weather but guitar solo wails; overall tone praised on several tracks.
band_member_comments.Phil: Dominates pre‑second‑vocal jam, initiates groove in the celebrated drum jam.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith's piano criticized as out of touch by some reviewers, while others defend his playing; Brent’s early electric piano tones described as inferior to later B3 work.
band_member_comments.Drums: Egyptian‑beat drum jam highlighted as a unique and powerful moment.
